course_description.overview: GFNY La Vaujany features a long competition course of ~150 km and a medium non-competitive course of 60.8 km, both starting in Vaujany and tackling the legendary Alpe d'Huez climb plus Vaujany ascent.
course_description.difficulty_narrative: Epic Alpine challenge with multiple HC climbs including Alpe d'Huez; long course for rankings, medium for finishers. Demands pacing for final Vaujany ascent.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Grenoble (~1 hour drive); arrive by Friday for prep. Bike service on race day.
logistics.accommodation: Vaujany offers options at all budget levels, restaurants, family activities.
logistics.registration_details: Register via gfny.com; expo Sat 9-19:00, Sun 6-7:00; chip timing.
logistics.bike_logistics: Bike service 7-7:45 race morning; supported group rides Fri/Sat for VIP.
logistics.local_considerations: French Alps; scenic village hosting. Post-race meal, awards at 14:00.

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic GFNY in French Alps conquering Alpe d'Huez and Vaujany.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Ambitious road cyclists seeking Tour de France climbs and qualifier spots.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the prestige and scenery of legendary Alpine roads.
pro_tips: Pace for final Vaujany climb after Alpe d'Huez.
Arrive Friday for acclimation and group rides.
Choose long course for rankings and NYC Worlds qualifier.
Book Vaujany lodging early for Alps base.
